title: Connect to an InfluxDB data source in Grafana Cloud

blocks.content:
This learning path shows you how to connect Grafana Cloud to an existing InfluxDB instance and query your time series data using Flux.

InfluxDB is a purpose-built time series database designed for high-throughput writes and fast queries over timestamped data. By connecting InfluxDB as a data source in Grafana, you can visualize and explore your InfluxDB data alongside other data sources.

### What you will learn

- When querying InfluxDB directly is the right choice versus remote-writing metrics
- How to verify your InfluxDB instance is accessible
- How to add and configure the InfluxDB data source in Grafana Cloud
- How to test the connection and explore your data using Flux queries

### Prerequisites

- An existing InfluxDB instance (v1.8+ or v2.x) accessible from Grafana Cloud
- InfluxDB connection details: URL, organization, API token, and default bucket
